title: Contribution Margin Analysis: Calculate the contribution margin ratio if the total sales are...
summary: Calculate the contribution margin ratio as the proportion of sales remaining after variable costs.
prompt: Calculate the contribution margin ratio if the total sales are $500,000 and variable costs are $300,000.
solution: Contribution Margin Ratio = (Sales - Variable Costs) / Sales = ($500,000 - $300,000) / $500,000 = 0.40 or 40%. This ratio aids in assessing profitability (Page 47).
